items.title: Explosion at China fireworks factory kills 26 people
items.summary: A blast at a fireworks factory in Hunan province has killed 26 people and injured 61, state media report.

items.title: Two killed and many injured after car driven into crowd in German city of Leipzig
items.summary: A 33-year-old German citizen was detained following the incident, the authorities say.

items.title: Second hantavirus case confirmed after three die in suspected cruise ship outbreak
items.summary: The operator of the MV Hondius ship says a Dutch couple and a German national have died.

items.title: The most talked-about looks at this year's Met Gala
items.summary: The theme for 2026 Met Gala fundraiser is "Costume Art", exploring fashion as an embodied art form.

items.title: Rescuers among three dead after yacht sinks off Australian coast
items.summary: Volunteer rescuers aged 78 and 62 died after their boat rolled during "treacherous" conditions.

items.title: A 'fun' superstar stuns rivals and reshapes politics in an Indian state
items.summary: Film star Vijay has defied naysayers to almost sweep Tamil Nadu polls, marking a break in the political order.

items.title: Popular Australian author pleads guilty over child exploitation material
items.summary: The Jasper Jones and Runt author was arrested in January during a police raid at his Perth home.

items.title: Deadly China plane crash was caused by fuel cut-off, says report
items.summary: The Boeing 737 plane crashed into a Chinese hillside in 2022, killing all 132 people on board.

items.title: Ukrainian drone hits upmarket Moscow high-rise ahead of Victory Day celebrations
items.summary: Russia has declared a unilateral ceasefire to cover the event and threatened a massive strike on central Kyiv if it were disrupted.

items.title: Three Russian diplomats expelled from Austria over spying accusations
items.summary: They gathered information using a "forest of antennas" installed on top of diplomatic buildings, the foreign minister says.

items.title: Mali junta leader names himself defence minister after predecessor killed
items.summary: Former Defence Minister Sadio Camara was killed in a massive offensive by combined jihadist and separatist forces.

items.title: Britney Spears pleads guilty to reckless driving after arrest
items.summary: The 44-year-old will avoid jail time after her arrest in California earlier this year.

items.title: Former New York City mayor Rudy Giuliani recovering from pneumonia and 'now breathing on his own'
items.summary: The ex-New York mayor 'remains in critical but stable condition', according to his spokesman.

items.title: Spain seizes record amount of cocaine in Atlantic Ocean, authorities say
items.summary: The Civil Guard found between 30,000 and 45,000kg of the drug on a freighter headed to Libya.

items.title: Modi's BJP conquers Bengal, one of India's toughest political frontiers
items.summary: West Bengal’s verdict could reshape not just the state’s politics, but the future trajectory of Modi’s BJP.

items.title: Robots move in as waste firms struggle to find staff
items.summary: Humanoid robots are being added to the automation of waste sorting.

items.title: Watch: Met Gala sees Beyoncé's return and a star-studded red carpet
items.summary: Anna Wintour brought the glam, Beyoncé brought the fam, and Heidi Klum was... statuesque at this year's fundraiser.

items.title: BBC uncovers the scammers exploiting dogs to elicit donations from animal lovers
items.summary: Unwitting donors hand over money to save suffering animals but Ugandan con artists pocket the cash.

items.title: Kenya battles to stop the 'goons and guns' as fears of political violence grow
items.summary: More than 100 gangs are operating nationwide as the growing use of political thugs raises fears of election violence.

items.title: Voters will judge Trump on the economy - how is it doing?
items.summary: How much Donald Trump can do with the time he has left as president will depend massively on the cost of living.

items.title: Laufey on making jazz cool again (and the fish that brought out her inner rage)
items.summary: The Icelandic star reflects on a phenomenal year, and the music video that let her go "primal".

items.title: Dragonflies in distress: Scientists sound alarm in India's ecological hotspot
items.summary: A new study reveals a decline in dragonfly and damselfly species in India's Western Ghats.

items.title: Do viruses spread more easily on cruise ships?
items.summary: The BBC explains what experts have to say about the infection risks associated with cruise ships.

items.title: Moment Indonesian river overflows from heavy rain
items.summary: A glamping facility was destroyed by the raging torrent in Bogor, West Java on 4 May.

items.title: Watch: Moment United Airlines flight strikes vehicle during landing
items.summary: Flight 169 from Venice, Italy, landed safely at Newark Airport with no reported injuries among the 231 passengers and crew. The lorry driver sustained minor injuries.

items.title: Huge crowd attends free Shakira Copacabana beach concert
items.summary: Shakira's appearance followed shows by Lady Gaga and Madonna, who have also performed on Copacabana beach in previous years.
